items.title: Page updated: U.S. Bankruptcy Court, District of Maine — Local Rules

items.summary:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.

What changed (text excerpt):
Added:
  + Where to File
  + Video/Telephonic Hearings
  + Court Holidays
  + Attorney Reference Guide
  + Local Rules
  + -
  + Links in the Table of Contents, when clicked, will take you to the corresponding rule
  + The Local Rules were last updated on September 28, 2021 amending Local Bankruptcy Rules 9013-1 and 9013-2. This updated version of the Local Rules includes all amendments through September 28, 2021, and is the official version of the Local Bankruptcy Rules for the District of Maine.
  + For Interim Rules implementing the Small Business Reorganization Act of 2019, please see the
  + Standing Order Regarding SBRA Interim Rules
  + .
  + Rules Amendments
  + The process for promulgating an amendment to a rule or form involves several levels of consideration and approval, the first of which is consideration and approval by the Local Rules Committee of the Bankruptcy Court. For a graphic representation of the rules amendment process, please see our
  + Local Rules Amendments flow chart
  + . Current local rule amendments published for public comment, if any, appear below. Proposed amendments are published for comment for 30 days. Comments should be sent to
  + Monica Bigley
  + , Clerk of Court (please make clear in the subject line of your email that the email is in regard to a proposed local rule).
  + Revision History
  + Commencing with revisions made on or after January 1, 2017, all amendments to the local rules (sorted by rule) can be found
  + here
  + .
  + 
[diff truncated]

items.title: Page updated: U.S. Bankruptcy Court, District of Maine — Local Rules

page_lines: Peter G. Cary, Chief U.S. Bankruptcy JudgeMichael A. Fagone, U.S. Bankruptcy JudgeMonica M. Bigley, Clerk of Court
How to Subscribe to the Court's RSS Feed
Pending Federal Rule and Form Amendments
Consistent with 28 U.S.C. § 2075 and Fed. R. Bankr. P. 9029(a), the Bankruptcy Court for the District of Maine has adopted local rules governing practice and procedure.
Summary of Changes to Local Bankruptcy Rules
Andrew M. Dudley, Standing Chapter 13 Trustee,
Ann Dirsa, Office of the U.S. Trustee,
Pending Federal Rule and Form Amendments
